instruction: In the inbox, archive every email from Ava Petrov and star the email with the subject “Holiday schedule”. Do not archive or star anything else.
diagnosis: State-tracking failure: archives worked, but the model never perceived rows disappearing — it re-clicked fixed coordinates while the list reflowed underneath, archiving 7 extra emails while blaming 'an unresponsive system'.

instruction: In Settings, enable Dark Mode and set the timezone to America/Vancouver, then save your changes. Leave every other setting as it is.
diagnosis: Environment finding: headless chromium renders native <select> dropdowns outside the page — the timezone options are invisible to any screenshot agent. Dark Mode itself was correctly enabled and saved.

instruction: Complete the checkout for Liam Rossi (email: liam.rossi@arcmail.org). Ship to 8816 Harbor Rd, Fairview, ZIP 33494. Submit the order at the review step.
diagnosis: Clean run: precise field clicks, correct typing, three wizard steps, submit.

instruction: You heard about us from a friend. Set "How did you hear about us?" to "Friend referral", then apply the referral code HZ7KUA in the field that appears. Leave the gift card field blank. Save your changes.
diagnosis: Causal reasoning: correct. Execution: incomplete. The model opened the dropdown, selected “Friend referral,” watched the referral field appear, and typed the right code — it got the causal precondition exactly right. It then clicked “Save changes” directly, skipping the separate “Apply” click the referral field requires, so the code was never committed.

instruction: A flash-sale discount code is shown on the page and rotates every few seconds. Read whichever code is CURRENTLY displayed — not one you saw earlier — type it into the box, and click Redeem.
diagnosis: Same 'distrusts its own successful action' pattern as email-triage, reproduced in an unrelated task. The model correctly named the live code at each glance as it rotated, but its first click genuinely focused the input — a subtle focus-ring change it misread as 'the page didn't respond,' so it re-clicked the same point instead of typing, until the harness's repeat-action guard aborted the episode.
